steps.title: Balance value and limitation
steps.content: The passage praises The Fifty-Year Mission as a comprehensive and valuable oral history, then pivots with "But" to name a limitation: it lacks a clear authorial point of view that could unify the many accounts. The main idea must include both the work's ambition and its flaw.

steps.title: Reject invented criticisms
steps.content: Choice A invents missing key events, while the text emphasizes broad coverage from every television series and film. Choice B invents a comparison between television and film accounts. Choice C invents low participation, though the passage says the book includes cast and crew accounts across the franchise. Choice D accurately presents the work as worthwhile and thorough while noting an important limitation in its approach. The answer is D.
